title: NOV Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend and Supplemental Dividend
author: Nov Inc.
description: NOV Inc. announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.09 per share payable on June 26, 2026, and a supplemental cash dividend of $0.09 per share payable on June 12, 2026, as part of its 2025 return of capital plan.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is returning capital to shareholders through both regular and supplemental dividends, demonstrating financial strength and confidence in its business. The declaration of a supplemental dividend in addition to the regular quarterly dividend indicates strong cash generation and commitment to shareholder value.

title: NOV Reports First Quarter 2026 Earnings
author: Na
description: NOV Inc. reported Q1 2026 revenues of $2.05 billion, down 2% year-over-year, with net income of $19 million ($0.05 per share). The company faced significant operational disruptions in the Middle East estimated at $54 million in revenue and $32 million in Adjusted EBITDA impact. Despite near-term challenges, management expects a shift toward an undersupplied market and anticipates a new capital equipment cycle driven by higher commodity prices and renewed energy security focus. The company returned $100 million to shareholders and secured several strategic contracts across offshore, drilling, and industrial applications.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed results with revenue decline and profitability pressure from Middle East disruptions, but offset by management's optimistic outlook on market recovery, strong order bookings ($520 million), and strategic contract wins. The company maintains solid backlog ($4.23 billion) and is investing in capacity expansion, suggesting confidence in future demand despite current headwinds.

title: NOV Appoints Sanjay Chowbey, President & CEO of Kennametal Inc., to the Board of Directors
author: Nov Inc.
description: NOV Inc. announced the appointment of Sanjay Chowbey, President and CEO of Kennametal Inc., to its Board of Directors effective March 17, 2026. Chowbey brings over 20 years of experience in global manufacturing and industrial technology leadership, with previous roles at Flowserve Corporation, Danaher Corporation, and Arvin Meritor. He will serve on NOV's Audit Committee.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The appointment of an experienced industrial executive with a strong track record in manufacturing and operational excellence to the board is viewed positively as it strengthens governance and is expected to drive shareholder value.

Kennametal is mentioned only as Chowbey's current employer. While his departure to join another board could be seen as a loss of leadership attention, the article contains no information suggesting negative impact on Kennametal's operations.

title: NOV Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Conference Call
author: Nov Inc.
description: NOV Inc. announced it will hold a conference call on April 28, 2026, at 10 a.m. Central Time to discuss its first quarter 2026 results. The company will issue a press release with results after market close on April 27, 2026, with the call webcast live on its investor website.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is a routine announcement of an earnings call and results release date. There is no information about financial performance, guidance, or business developments that would indicate positive or negative sentiment. It is a standard procedural announcement.

title: NOV Announces 20 Percent Increase in Regular Quarterly Dividend
author: Globe Newswire
description: NOV Inc. announced that its Board of Directors voted to increase its regular quarterly cash dividend by 20 percent to $0.09 per share. The dividend is payable on March 27, 2026 to stockholders of record on March 13, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced a 20% increase in its regular quarterly dividend, demonstrating confidence in financial performance and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Dividend increases are typically viewed positively by investors as they signal strong cash flow and management confidence in future earnings.
